misconceptions.title: Graph Inequalities Incorrect Slope
misconceptions.description: When graphing inequalities, students interpret the inequality without converting it to slope-intercept form first. This mistake leads to choosing a boundary line with the wrong slope and incorrect graphing.

misconceptions.title: Boundary Line Solid/Dash Confusion
misconceptions.description: Students use a solid line for strict inequalities or misinterpret the inequality symbols, leading to an incorrect boundary line inclusion.
successIndicators: Distinguish between strict and inclusive symbols
Draw dash line for strict inequalities
Use solid line for inclusive inequalities

misconceptions.title: Incorrect Variable Substitution in Systems
misconceptions.description: When solving systems, students find variables correctly but substitute incorrectly into equations, leading to wrong final values.

misconceptions.title: Mistake in Clearing Fractions
misconceptions.description: An incorrect method is used to clear fractions from equations, leading to wrong equivalent equations when trying to solve for variables.
successIndicators: Multiply each term by common denominator
